policy.sameSegment: Beaches in the same curated shoreline segment should render the same visible exposure level unless a verified local morphology or wind-sport exception locks a stricter level.
policy.confidence: Segment propagation may support map-color consistency and medium review, but it never creates high confidence by itself.

segments.notes: Megali Ammos, Agios Charalampos, and Agia Anna are neighboring northwest-facing Chora-side records; dirt-road/access handling remains separate.
Agia Anna Paragkas, Paragka, and Kalamopodi form a close south-facing resort-front; Platys Gialos and Psarrou are kept separate because west-wind exposure differs.
Elia, Gymnistiki Paralia Elias, and Agrari are adjacent south-facing records with near-identical orientation.
Kalafati and Agia Anna Kalafatis are neighboring southeast/south-facing Kalafatis bay records with similar exposure behavior.
Agios Stefanos and Choulakia are nearby southwest-facing northwest-coast records with similar practical exposure behavior.
Panormos and Agios Sostis are north/northeast-facing Panormos-bay records; amenities and popularity remain separate.
Fokos and Mersini are neighboring north-facing remote records; difficult/passable road access must not become a top-pick shortcut.
Merchia and Sokratis are nearby northeast-facing records with matching shoreline behavior; access confidence remains separate.
